summary: Completed comprehensive testing of CropSense AI app. Frontend is fully functional with all UI components working correctly. Backend has one critical issue with HuggingFace API integration, but Claude AI advisory is working properly.

critical_bugs.issue: HuggingFace API returning HTML error pages instead of JSON. Tried multiple model endpoints but all failing with authentication or model access issues.
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks disease classification functionality - core feature of the app
critical_bugs.details: Tested both api-inference.huggingface.co and router.huggingface.co endpoints. May need valid HF_TOKEN or different model selection.

working_endpoints.status: Both GET and POST working correctly
Working correctly with Claude AI integration. Successfully handles array-to-string conversion for treatment and preventive measures.

integration_issues.issue: Will fail when users try to analyze images due to backend /api/classify endpoint failure

passed_tests: Landing page loads correctly with all sections
Hero section displays stats (95.41% accuracy, 38 classes, ~5s diagnosis, Free)
Navigation between landing page and app page works
Upload panel with drag-drop zone renders correctly
Crop selector chips (Tomato, Potato, Wheat, Corn, Apple, Pepper, Other) all functional
Results panel shows empty state correctly
How It Works section with 3 step cards displays properly
Pipeline visualization with 5 steps renders correctly
Analyze button properly disabled when no image uploaded
Claude AI advisory endpoint working with proper JSON response formatting
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix HuggingFace API integration in /api/classify endpoint. The current HF_TOKEN may be invalid or the model endpoints are not accessible. Consider: 1) Verify HF_TOKEN validity, 2) Try alternative plant disease classification models from HuggingFace Hub, 3) Implement fallback or mock classification for testing purposes.
